data.title: Stock index futures tick higher after rally driven by U.S.-U.K. trade deal
data.description:
Stock index futures inched up a day after Wall Street rallied following the announcement of a trade deal between the U.S. and the United Kingdom, while investors hope for more such deals. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.3%, and Dow futures (INDU) were flat.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.38%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 1 basis point to 3.87%.

Wall Street on Thursday rose after President Donald Trump exhorted traders to “go out and buy stock now” after announcing a trade deal between the U.S. and the United Kingdom—the first of its kind since Trump's imposition of sweeping reciprocal tariffs in April.

“U.S. consumers are worse off than in January, though better off than a week ago. Some U.K. exporters get better conditions, some U.S. exporters might get better conditions. Yesterday’s Bank of England's rate cut was a more important economic event,” UBS' Paul Donovan said.

In more tariff news, the Trump administration is reportedly considering a significant reduction in tariffs during weekend talks with China, aiming to ease tensions and mitigate the economic strain both countries are beginning to experience.

The economic calendar is light on Friday, with mainly Federal Reserve speakers lined up. 

Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee, New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams, and Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Beth Hammack are all scheduled to speak.

data.title: Commodity Roundup: Crude oil set for weekly gains amid signs of easing U.S.-China trade tensions
data.description:
Oil futures rose on Friday, on their way to break a two-week losing streak, as trade tensions between top oil consumers, U.S. and China eased, while overall market sentiment improved.

Brent crude (CO1:COM) rose 1.6% to $63.4 a barrel at the time of writing, WTI (CL1:COM) up 1.8% to $60.96.

U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent is set to meet with China's top economic official, Vice Premier He Lifeng, in Switzerland on May 10 to address trade disputes that have posed risks to crude oil consumption growth.

Sources familiar with the preparations told Bloomberg that the U.S. has set an initial goal of cutting tariffs to below 60%, a move they believe China may be willing to reciprocate.

Also, high on the US wish list is securing the removal of China’s export restrictions on rare earths used to make magnets, as a range of industries face disruption.

"Easing trade tensions was the predominant red thread through markets this past week, with the key being the weekend meeting between US and Chinese trade negotiators in Geneva," Ole Hansen, Head of Commodity Strategy at Saxo Bank, said.

Following a challenging April, the commodities sector has been finding its feet again, with early May action driving a 1.4% gain, led by pro-cyclical and growth-dependent sectors, Hansen added.

"On an individual level, losses were led by corn, coffee, and copper, with gains at the other end being led by European and US natural gas (NG1:COM), crude oil, and gasoline."

Precious metals meanwhile, including silver (XAGUSD:CUR), continue to attract Chinese investors and some haven demand, as geopolitical risks persist—most recently between India and Pakistan.

Spot gold (XAUUSD:CUR) rose 0.3% to $3,326.25 an ounce, up 2% for the week, with the safe-haven asset trading sharply higher after last week's correction attempt failed to get any traction. 

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI)

More Gold and Gold Miner ETFs: (IAU), (SGOL), (OUNZ), (BAR), (GDX), (GDXJ), (NUGT), (RING), (DUST)

data.title: Chimera highlights 9.2% economic return and $187M liquidity boost in Q1 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Chimera Investment Corporation (CIM) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Phil Kardis highlighted the company's performance improvements, including a 7.4% increase in book value and a 9.2% economic return for the quarter. He emphasized the seamless integration of Palisades, which has contributed to a 43% year-over-year growth in third-party loans under management, now totaling nearly $24 billion.
  • Kardis also noted impactful balance sheet moves, such as exercising call rights on non-Remic securitizations and issuing two new securitizations, unlocking $187 million at a reinvestment hurdle below 6%. Additional activities included acquiring and securitizing $288 million in non-QM loans and purchasing $149 million in agency specified pools.
  • CFO Subra Viswanathan reported GAAP net income of $145.9 million, or $1.77 per share, and a GAAP book value of $21.17 per share. Earnings available for distribution were $33.5 million, or $0.41 per share. Economic net interest income was $72.3 million, with a net interest spread of 1.5%.
  • Viswanathan explained the company’s liquidity position, with $697 million in total cash and unencumbered assets, and detailed refinancing activities that extracted over $100 million of additional cash.

Outlook

  • Management projected a stable to slightly declining book value in Q2 2025, estimating a decrease of about 40 basis points as of early May. They expect continued diversification of the portfolio, growth in recurring fee income, and enhanced liquidity.
  • Kardis indicated plans to deploy capital tactically, focusing on agency RMBS and liquidity reserves, with approximately 40% of the $187 million from re-securitization activities already allocated.
  • The company aims to further expand its third-party asset management business and explore opportunities in mortgage servicing rights (MSRs) to diversify revenue and hedge portfolio risks.

Financial Results

  • The company reported a GAAP net income of $145.9 million and economic net interest income of $72.3 million for Q1 2025. The quarterly dividend per common share was $0.37, contributing to the economic return of 9.2%.
  • Total leverage was 3.9 to 1, with recourse leverage at 1.2 to 1. The yield on average interest-earning assets was 5.9%, while the average cost of funds stood at 4.4%.
  • Refinancing activities during the quarter included the execution of $155 million pay-fixed Eris Swap futures and a $1 billion two-year SOFR interest rate cap at a 3.95% strike.

Q&A

  • Doug Harter, UBS: Asked about the timing for deploying the $187 million investment capital and future dividend considerations. Kardis responded that about 40% of the capital is already allocated, with the remainder to be deployed tactically over the next 4 to 8 weeks. Dividend decisions will depend on market conditions in the coming months.
  • Trevor Cranston, Citizens JMP: Queried on the growth outlook for the third-party asset management business. Kardis expressed optimism, citing potential for growth through new clients and increased activity with existing clients.
  • Bose George, KBW: Asked about book value performance and portfolio duration post-hedge roll-offs. Kardis explained that book value stability was affected by offsetting changes in loan values and securitized debt performance.
  • Eric Hagen, BTIG: Inquired about sensitivity to higher delinquency rates in the RPL and non-QM portfolios. Kardis emphasized stable delinquency trends in the RPL portfolio and manageable risks in non-QM loans due to strong borrower equity and credit fundamentals.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised targeted questions about capital deployment, portfolio risks, and earnings potential, reflecting a slightly cautious but overall neutral sentiment.
  • Management demonstrated confidence in their strategic initiatives and financial performance, particularly in the integration of Palisades and the strengthening of the balance sheet. Statements such as "we are not just playing defense, we're building Chimera into a hybrid mortgage REIT that's resilient and diversified" conveyed a positive tone.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment remained consistent, focusing on potential risks and opportunities, while management's tone showed increased confidence, supported by tangible improvements in key metrics.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Book value increased 7.4% in Q1 2025 compared to a 3% estimated increase at the end of Q4 2024.
  • Economic return rose to 9.2% in Q1 2025, a significant improvement from the -10.1% economic return reported in Q4 2024.
  • Management’s confidence in expanding fee-based revenue streams and leveraging Palisades was more pronounced in Q1 2025.
  • Analysts continued to emphasize book value stability and portfolio risk management in both quarters, with a more pronounced focus on capital deployment in Q1 2025.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged ongoing market volatility and potential spread widening as challenges but emphasized the company’s strong liquidity position and disciplined risk management approach.
  • Analysts raised concerns about delinquency risks, book value volatility, and the impact of macroeconomic conditions on securitization markets.

Final Takeaway

Chimera Investment Corporation showcased robust performance in Q1 2025, marked by a 9.2% economic return and a 7.4% increase in book value. Strategic initiatives, including the integration of Palisades and impactful refinancing activities, strengthened the company’s liquidity and portfolio diversification. Management remains focused on leveraging growth opportunities in fee-based revenue and MSRs while navigating market volatility with a disciplined approach.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Onestream targets 20% revenue growth for 2025 amid rising demand for finance AI solutions
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: OneStream, Inc. (OS) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Tom Shea highlighted a strong start to 2025, with total revenue growing by 24% and a free cash flow margin of 26%. He emphasized resilience amid macroeconomic challenges and the increasing importance of system modernization for CFOs and finance departments.
  • SensibleAI Forecast, the company’s machine-learning product, experienced over 50% year-over-year bookings growth. The product is central to OneStream’s Finance AI portfolio and is increasingly integrated into customer workflows.
  • The company released its CPM Express product, which offers rapid implementation solutions for businesses of various sizes, aiming to broaden the customer base.
  • A key international win included a European retail group replacing legacy systems with OneStream's platform, contributing to 40% year-over-year growth in the international segment.
  • CFO Bill Koefoed reported total revenue of $136 million, subscription revenue of $125 million, and a record free cash flow of $36 million for the quarter. International revenue contributed more than 30% of the total.

Outlook

  • Management reiterated its guidance for 2025, targeting total revenue between $583 million and $587 million, representing 20% growth year-over-year. Non-GAAP operating margin guidance was slightly increased.
  • For Q2 2025, revenue guidance is set between $140 million and $142 million, with non-GAAP net income per share expected between $0.00 and $0.02.
  • Key growth drivers include the expansion of the Finance AI portfolio and the CPM Express product, along with strategic investments in international markets.

Financial Results

  • Total revenue for Q1 2025 was $136 million, with subscription revenue growing by 31% year-over-year to $125 million.
  • Free cash flow reached a record $36 million, representing a 26% free cash flow margin.
  • Non-GAAP net income for the quarter was $6.7 million, with earnings per share of $0.04.
  • International revenue saw significant growth of 40% year-over-year, reflecting successful market penetration in global regions.

Q&A

  • John DiFucci, Guggenheim Securities, inquired about guidance conservatism. CFO Koefoed noted the positive indicators in the sales pipeline but emphasized prudence due to macroeconomic uncertainties.
  • Adam Hotchkiss, Goldman Sachs, asked about CPM Express adoption. CEO Shea stated that the product’s faster time-to-value approach has been well-received and aligns with customer demand for efficient solutions.
  • Terry Tillman, Truist Securities, questioned SensibleAI Forecast’s growth trajectory. CEO Shea highlighted the transformative potential of AI-driven solutions in finance, citing increased adoption and customer expansion as key success indicators.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about macroeconomic uncertainties but acknowledged the company’s strong pipeline and product innovation as positive offsets.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing growth opportunities in Finance AI and international markets. CEO Shea reiterated the company’s long-term vision and operational discipline.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone reflected cautious optimism, focusing on execution amid external challenges.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue growth decelerated from 29% in Q4 2024 to 24% in Q1 2025, reflecting seasonality and macroeconomic factors.
  • International revenue growth remained robust at 40% year-over-year, consistent with prior performance.
  • Management’s tone shifted slightly, with increased emphasis on macroeconomic prudence and strategic investments.
  • Analysts continued to focus on AI solutions and international expansion as key growth drivers, aligning with prior quarter themes.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged potential deal headwinds and budget constraints due to macroeconomic uncertainties and government restructuring.
  • Ongoing customer SaaS conversions are expected to impact license revenue. Currency fluctuations also pose a risk to international revenue.
  • Analysts expressed concerns regarding the cautious revenue guidance and its implications for broader market conditions.

Final Takeaway

OneStream showcased strong momentum in Q1 2025, driven by growth in its Finance AI solutions and international markets. The company reiterated its revenue growth target of 20% for 2025 and highlighted opportunities in system modernization and AI-driven forecasting. Despite macroeconomic challenges, OneStream’s strategic investments and robust pipeline position it for continued progress in the modern finance landscape.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
